##NAMES=Hurlbutite
##RRUFFID=R070612
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=CaBe_2_(PO_4_)_2_
##LOCALITY=Viitaniemi, Erajarvi, Orivesi, Etela-Suomen Laani, Finland
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=James Shigley
##DESCRIPTION=Light brown, massive, associated with beryllonite, hydroxylherderite, and apatite. The hurlbutite is very fine-grained holding the other coarser mineral fragments in place.
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R070612
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 8.313 b: 8.794 c: 7.818 alpha: 90 beta: 90.6 gamma: 90 volume: 571.6 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
